codeReview.summary: The LLM integration in CoderAgent.execute() is correctly implemented. The code properly checks for LLM availability, builds a well-structured prompt requesting JSON with new_content field, handles both successful LLM responses with valid JSON and various failure modes (invalid JSON, LLM errors), and falls back to heuristic approach when LLM is unavailable. The diff generation logic is preserved and works correctly with the new content.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker modified multiple files beyond the assigned feature scope. The feature was specifically 'fix-coder-agent-llm' targeting CoderAgent, but the commit shows changes to execution.rs, scheduler.rs (orchestrator), sqlite.rs (state), and changes in other crates (swell-llm, swell-tools, swell-state). The AGENTS.md documents the feature-based workflow but doesn't explicitly require atomic commits per feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 7b83c56 --stat shows: crates/swell-orchestrator/src/agents.rs, execution.rs, scheduler.rs, crates/swell-state/src/sqlite.rs, and others modified. Feature ID is fix-coder-agent-llm but changes span multiple subsystems.

summary: The fix-coder-agent-llm feature successfully integrates the LLM backend into CoderAgent.execute(). When self.llm is Some, it now calls llm.chat() with a properly formatted prompt requesting JSON with new_content field, parses the response, and uses the returned content. When LLM is unavailable or fails, it correctly falls back to the heuristic approach. All 5 CoderAgent tests pass. The implementation meets the expected behavior requirements.
